title: Cloud Service Models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)

sections.title: Understanding Service Models
sections.content:
Cloud computing offers three main service models, each providing different levels of control, flexibility, and management. Think of them as different levels of a restaurant experience:

- **IaaS** = Grocery store (you control everything)
- **PaaS** = Meal kit service (some prep done for you)
- **SaaS** = Restaurant (everything done for you)

sections.title: IaaS - Infrastructure as a Service
sections.content:
**What is IaaS?**
IaaS provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. You rent servers, storage, and networking instead of buying physical hardware.

**What YOU manage:**
- Operating System (Windows, Linux, etc.)
- Applications and software
- Data and databases
- Security configurations

**What PROVIDER manages:**
- Physical servers and data centers
- Networking hardware
- Storage systems
- Virtualization layer
examples: **Amazon EC2** - Virtual servers in the cloud

sections.title: PaaS - Platform as a Service
sections.content:
**What is PaaS?**
PaaS provides a platform for developers to build, run, and manage applications without dealing with infrastructure. You focus on your code, the platform handles the rest.

**What YOU manage:**
- Your application code
- Your data
- Application configuration

**What PROVIDER manages:**
- Operating system
- Runtime environment (Python, Java, Node.js)
- Web servers
- Databases
- Networking
- Physical infrastructure
examples: **AWS Elastic Beanstalk** - Deploy apps without managing servers
**Google App Engine** - Build scalable web apps

sections.title: SaaS - Software as a Service
sections.content:
**What is SaaS?**
SaaS delivers software applications over the internet on a subscription basis. You just use the software - everything else is managed by the provider.

**What YOU manage:**
- Your data only
- User access and permissions
- Application settings/preferences

**What PROVIDER manages:**
- The entire software application
- Infrastructure
- Platform
- Security
- Updates and patches

sections.title: Comparison Chart
sections.content: Who manages what in each service model:

advantages: Choose the right level of control for your needs
IaaS offers maximum flexibility and control
Mix and match models for different workloads
